title: ALL CAPS: THE HISTORY OF THE AMERICAN BASEBALL HAT
chaptersLine: Forty-plus chapters drafted, plus an introduction and a closing coda.
spanLine: From before the Civil War to the drops of 2026.
thisWeek: Folded in a long sit-down with one of the makers who actually manufactures the custom caps.
Ran down a century-old language curiosity and confirmed it, without giving away where it lands.
Filed three brand-new 2026 releases as live evidence the story is still being written in real time.
chapterHighlights: The pre-Civil War straw hats and top hats Americans wore long before the ballcap existed.
The 1870s mail-order catalog cap that quietly set the template every fitted still follows.
The one team's cap that slipped loose of baseball and ended up on heads that never watch a game.
The surf-shop signature that pulled the ballcap out of the dugout and into streetwear.
